Cooking/Cooking-Index.title: Cooking Index
Cooking/Cooking-Index.content:
I eat an unhealthy amount of eggs. I should branch out, but…
Omelette

Scrambled Eggs

Cooking/Omelette.title: Omelette
Cooking/Omelette.content:
Thick omelette with fillings, folded once and garnished on a plate.
Whole meal for one person, or cut in half to be two meal parts.
Utensils

 bowl / mug
 whisk
 flat spatula
 pan
 plate

Ingredients

 eggs 3-4
 milk 1 tsp
 olive oil 1 tbsp
 pepper flakes / powder 1 tsp
 herbes de Provence 1-2 tsp
 filling of your choice (pepperoni, bacon bits, etc.)
 grated cheese
 ketchup

Instructions

in pan, pour oil at 50% heat
in bowl / mug, whisk eggs, milk, pepper, and herbs until smooth
in pan, pour bowl
with spatula, push mix around until no liquid remains
add cheese and filling, cook until melted
fold once, optionally divide once with spatula
on plate, serve with ketchup drizzle and herbs

Cooking/Scrambled-Eggs.title: Scrambled Eggs
Cooking/Scrambled-Eggs.content:
Quick and easy scrambled eggs, easy on the mouth.
Served to your heart’s content, moreso as a meal part.
Utensils

 bowl / mug
 whisk
 flat spatula
 pan
 plate

Ingredients

 eggs 2-4
 milk 1 tsp
 olive oil 1 tbsp
 pepper flakes / powder 1 tsp
 herbes de Provence 1-2 tsp
 grated cheese
 ketchup

Instructions

in pan, pour oil at 50% heat
in bowl / mug, whisk eggs, milk, pepper, and herbs until smooth
in pan, pour bowl
with spatula, constantly break up forming egg until no liquid remains
mix around and break up remaining egg into small pieces
add cheese, mix and cook until melted
on plate, serve with ketchup drizzle and herbs

Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Amphi.title: Amphi
Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Amphi.content:
Amphi (she/it) is an Octoling with pink ink and tan skin. Her eyes are dark pink. She has the Tentacurl hairstyle with dark gray ends, and classic eyebrows. It wears translucent SV925 Circle Shades, a custom yellow shirt with text inside a red splat, green loose denim shorts, Punk Pinks, and wields an Inkbrush.
It’s a member of Fresh Outta Hell.

Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Calcutta.title: Calcutta
Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Calcutta.content:
A cut above the rest.

- Calcutta, choosing a future epitaph for herself

Calcutta [kalkiuta] or [kalkɜta] (she/her) is an Extrainkadian (or Neukadian), quickly recognizable from her inkless hair with a resemblance to petroleum, and for bearing features of Inklings and Octolings alike, not falling cleanly into either species. She has the Banger hairstyle and pointy ears from Inklings, the left ear indent and square fingertips from Octolings, and grey reflective irises. She lacks eyebrows.
As her hair lacks ink, its bioluminescent attributes are much easier to see. Usually appearing black in dark areas, sufficient light reveals its true appearance, a white spotty translucence, drained of any color.
Note the black appearance in dark areas, the spotty white translucence in bright areas, and the noticeable bioluminescent spots on her bangs.
Her Event outfit, used in all timelines, is comprised solely of modified gear, notably Air Gills DX, a Splatjack 5000, and Angry Rain Boots. Her mask is watertight, so her words have to come out through small speakers installed within. The quality is not great, but allows an interesting fear factor.
Her Chill outfit was made by Miwa, so she could have proper, non-threatening clothes to visit the Inkadian cities. Suede Gray Lace-Ups and a Tumeric Zekko Coat make her look a lot less scary, and lets her keep a waterproof article of clothing so she isn’t too uncomfortable.
Calcutta aims to bring about Total Immersion, a theoretical event where the world would be suffocated in eternal rains and rising seas, preventing ink from prevailing, and undoing as much intelligent life as possible.
Knowingly, this includes her.
Although her plans are intently harmful to all, Calcutta goes out of her way to not kill anyone herself, unless necessary. In her mind, her plans are not sadistic, moreso a gift of gentle rest. In the timeline where she meets Miwa, she even makes her an affectionate gift with genuine intent, something she has never done for anyone before, showing she has empathy, and truly does believe her actions are righteous.


                  
                  Meta Storytelling 
                  
                
Calcutta is, on a personal level, about my moral growth in life.
To her, the world is war, and nothing else matters. She only sees the conflict, misery, and struggle everyone faces and, most importantly, causes. She believes people will always make themselves suffer, and there is no way to grow out of this pattern. The only way is Total Immersion. Annihilation.
In her canon ending, Calcutta faces the world with all her might, and is brought down unceremoniously, left to be forgotten by the few who even acknowledged her. Of course, this is a sad and uncomfortable ending. It isn’t satisfying. It isn’t fair. It’s exactly how it should be.
Despite this, there are people who hear this, and wish it could be different. And so, they design their own characters, their own stories, their own chance at changing fate. Despite everything she could do, there will be people wishing she could be happy, warm, and loved.
Because it isn’t about what she could do.
It’s about what she will.
No matter how deep down I was in my nihilism, my fears, my hate, there were people who became my friends, my very close friends. People I could never live without. They let me see that, maybe, there is warmth in the world. There is something other than conflict, than misery, than struggle.
Calcutta above the rest.
But it doesn’t have to be this way.

Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Charlotte's-Spl.title: Charlotte's Splatsona
Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Charlotte's-Spl.content:
Charlotte’s Splatsona is an Octoling with purple ink and light skin. Their eyes have seaweed-green sclerae and black pupils. They have a custom hairstyle, and thin eyebrows. They wear a Koshien Bandana, grey Li’l Devil Horns, and an Annaki Blue Cuff.
They are a member of Fresh Outta Hell.

Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Karcy.title: Karcy
Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Karcy.content:
Karcy (she/they) is an Octoling with purple ink and light skin. Her eyes have star-shaped pupils. She has a custom hairstyle (Tentatwists as a fallback), and thick eyebrows. She wears a Short Beanie, a Grape Hoodie, loose sport shorts, and Black Trainers. She wields a Splatana Stamper Nouveau, fitting for a berserker. Although their fine motor skills leave much to be desired, they are strangely graceful with large, heavy, cumbersome weapons. Their clumsiness is a blessing in disguise, leading her to prefer face-to-face encounters. Her laid-back nature and aloofness can be troublesome in serious situations, but she isn’t one to give up!
They are a member of Fresh Outta Hell.

Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Lune.title: Lune
Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Lune.content:
Lune [lyn] (xe/her) is an Octoling with black ink, olive skin, and yellow eyes. Xe has a custom hairstyle  covering the left side of her face (Tentatwists as a fallback), and thick eyebrows. Xe wears Hipster Horn-Rims, a Hero Jacket Replica, Black Dakroniks, Sarouel pants, and xe wields an N-ZAP ‘85.
Feisty! Would never refuse a challenge, or a meal. Xe is a member of Fresh Outta Hell.

Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Maeryn.title: Maeryn
Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Maeryn.content:
Maeryn (cos/they/it) is an Inkling with yellow ink and light skin. Cos eyes have deep blue sclerae and pupils, with thin white irises. Co has a custom hairstyle (Bed Head as a fallback), and classic eyebrows. Co wears Cephalo Pods, a Lemon Mountain Coat, patterned leggings, Onyx 01STERs, and wields a one-handed Splatana Wiper Deco.
Quiet, but not reserved. Terse in tone and diction, Maeryn is approachable and happy to help, not hesitating to take initiative. Prone to harsh anger when losing cos cool.
Co is a member of Fresh Outta Hell.

Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Miwa.title: Miwa
Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Miwa.content:
Miwa (she/her) is an Inkling with green ink, light skin, and green eyes. She has a custom hairstyle (Megalobraid as a fallback) and thin eyebrows. She wields two Splattershots, despite the accuracy loss.
Her Chill outfit is an Annaki Evolution Tee, Black Dakronics, and loose denim shorts.
She is a young and hopeful lady with a fighting spirit and a warm heart. She has a close relationship with Calcutta, being the only one to know her at all. Miwa is her only unbiased exposure to the world and of Inkling society. However, she remains unaware of her friend’s goals to achieve Total Immersion, and how it will inevitably harm both of them.
Despite everything, Miwa enjoys Calcutta’s company, and loves handholding her around the world she is so used to, giving her tours of the city. While Calcutta is unknowingly taught information she could use for ill intent, Miwa is a strong enough influence that her friend allows herself to be guided through the pretty night lights of the breathing city.
She was born with buckteeth, so she cannot close her mouth fully, leaving a thin gap, not that she minds. Calcutta finds it cute.

In the Miwa’s Choice Timeline, she has to face Calcutta, and hides her face as best she can, so as to avoid being recognized.


                  
                  Todo: Agent 10 outfit 
                  
                

 headgear (Annaki Mask, Octoleet Goggles)
 clothes (Airflow & Hustle Jacket)
 pants
 shoes
 weapon (Splat Dualies instead of usual akimbo Splattershot)

Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Rain's-Splatson.title: Rain's Splatsona
Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Rain's-Splatson.content:
Rain’s Splatsona is an Octoling with grey ink and pale skin. Their eyes have red sclerae, and black diplopia pupils. They have a large custom hairstyle (Octolocks or Tentacurl as a fallback), reminiscent of the Wave and Hipster hairstyles, and thin eyebrows. They wear something similar to Neo Octoling Armor or a Front-Zip Vest, and they wield an E-Liter 4K.
They are a member of Fresh Outta Hell.

Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Smoky's-Splatso.title: Smoky's Splatsona
Splatoon/Lore/Characters/Smoky's-Splatso.content:
Smoky’s Splatsona is a black bear, employed as an Inventory Manager at Grizzco Industries. Safety regulations be damned, if you want an automatic slosher, you’ll get an automatic slosher. Their know-how and risk-taking got them hired at a high rank of management with ease.
In the Fresh Outta Hell Timeline, rather than throw away broken tech, they sell it to Calcutta, at a very worthwhile price. They do not know nor care what purpose she will use these resources for. This makes them the only individual to know her before her attempt at Total Immersion.

Splatoon/Lore/Concepts/Fresh-Outta-Hell.title: Fresh Outta Hell
Splatoon/Lore/Concepts/Fresh-Outta-Hell.content:
A group of tightly-knit friends, budding talents, aiming to reach the skies of turf wars and bangers alike!
They are present in the Fresh Outta Hell Timeline, where they are trapped in the city during its assault. With all their bravery (or foolishness), they take matters in their own hands, before an Agent could get to the scene and deal with it themselves. Five on one, surely they can save the day! Surely.
This group is comprised of:

Alice’s Splatsona
Amphi
Ashley’s Splatsona
Charlotte’s Splatsona
Karcy
Lune
Maeryn
Rain’s Splatsona

Splatoon/Lore/Timeline/Calcutta-Alone-Ti.title: Calcutta Alone Timeline
Splatoon/Lore/Timeline/Calcutta-Alone-Ti.content:
In this canon timeline, Calcutta will never meet anyone until the time she assaults Inkopolis. At that moment, an unspecified agent will come to strike her down. This timeline resolves through two paths.
1. Calcutta dies
If Calcutta dies, her machines will be safely disconstructed and removed, allowing peace to be restored as though nothing had ever happened. Her body will remain, until it is discarded, too. There will be no legacy but a mere memory of a particularly bad storm, back then, in Inkopolis.
2. Calcutta thrives
If the agent fails to kill Calcutta, she may not necessarily kill them. Regardless of that choice, Total Immersion will begin, harder to stop as it progresses, until nobody can do anything about it. The world will be submerged, despite its struggle to live, into a gentle death.
Nobody is happy with this.

Splatoon/Lore/Timeline/Fresh-Outta-Hell-.title: Fresh Outta Hell Timeline
Splatoon/Lore/Timeline/Fresh-Outta-Hell-.content:
In this alternate timeline, Calcutta meets Fresh Outta Hell, a tightly knit aspiring group.


                  
                  Warning
                  
                
This page is still a draft. Expect missing or out-of-date information.


Calcutta is defeated by FOH

Calcutta is taken down
Calcutta is given a chance to join the Inkadian world


FOH is defeated by Calcutta

TotImm is enacted, Calcutta is joined by a custom Parallel Canon of familiar-looking bodies

Splatoon/Lore/Timeline/Miwa's-Choice-Tim.title: Miwa's Choice Timeline
Splatoon/Lore/Timeline/Miwa's-Choice-Tim.content:
In this canon timeline, Calcutta meets Miwa, affecting the way she sees the world whether she likes it or not. However, regardless of what happens, she will proceed with her plans, with Miwa will be the one to try and stop her. Her identity being hidden under the mask, Calcutta will not know who she’s up against until it’s too late.
This timeline can resolve through these paths:
1. Calcutta dies by Miwa’s hands
Miwa takes Calcutta down, keeping her mask up so she won’t be recognize her. To Calcutta, she is dying at the hands of a stranger. To Miwa, she is killing the person she cared for the most.
After all, the best agent for the job would be someone closest to the target, already aware of their patterns, strategies, morals, and weaknesses.
1.1 Miwa takes her role
Having proven herself, Miwa joins the New Squidbeak Splatoon, becoming Agent 10. She is seen in high regard, having stopped a great menace on her own. It doesn’t feel worth it.
1.2 Miwa rejects her role
From spite and loss, Miwa denies her role as agent, and decides to turn against the New Squidbeak Splatoon. She wishes to take them all down, for what they have made her do.
2. Miwa dies by Calcutta’s hands
Despite her best efforts, Agent 10 is brought down, failing her mission. As she dies, Calcutta is now able to approach, and learn she was fighting with Miwa all along. Now, nothing can be undone.
2.1 Calcutta lets herself die
Having lost the only person she has ever had, Calcutta lets go of her projects, thus of her only remaining purpose. An old cassette is left to play into the city’s speakers while she leaves to the water, still wearing her heavy gear.
2.2 Calcutta gets worse
Grief fueling her rage, Calcutta decides her morals are to be challenged. From then on, she will not hesitate to harm people if it’ll get her what she wants. Nothing will stop her this time. Nobody will stop her. Nobody.

Writings/Alk'ost.title: Alk'ost
Writings/Alk'ost.content:
Alk’ost Survival Apparatus

”I am one of many, but we are all the same.”

In the fresh ruins of villages and cities, a pseudomechanical array of legs carry and feed a large egg-shaped capsule almost meant to be see-through, whirring and struggling with every twist of its manyfold joints.
As a self-given goal, it ravages constructs and withdraws flesh and blood, meant to keep the dormant being in its mechanical heart.
The caregiven’s body is long dead, yet something must fuel the machine itself. You could suspect a symbiotic relationship, but the only intended means of opening the spider-like shell will surely kill it.

”There is nothing worth more than your objective. Value yourself and your mission above all else. This is how you will thrive.”

Alk’ost machines abuse turn economy in their own way. They bear many limbs, and each limb has its own health. All connected to a core, that core will always delegate damage taken to the limb with the lowest health if possible.
An Alk’ost machine may dedicate a number of limbs to an action, packing them together for a turn. Damage taken by a limb not only detaches it from its pack, but that damage is duplicated (instead of distributed) to other limbs it was packed with, reducing their effective health as more limbs act together.
Depending on its means of function, an Alk’ost machine may require to keep a number of limbs busy at all times. For example, Survival Apparatus is made of twenty limbs, and requires a pack of at least four under itself to move. If this quota is not respected, their function fails, letting the core fall to the ground, now in peril until next turn where it will be able to dedicate other limbs to the task.
As they are truly mechanical beings, Alk’ost entities do not experience any harm that would be a threat to organic folks. Depending on their composition, they may ignore certain afflictions meant to cull them, such as spells with Rust in their name.
However, due to the nature of their core, psychic harm is not out of the question if you only care to pass through. You’re not above their morals either way.
As a common rule, Necrotic and Radiant methods are vain in their entirety, having strictly no effect on them. Psychic and Lighting, however, are worryingly efficient.
Of course, each Alk’ost machine is unique, as their core makes it impossible to mass-produce. I refuse to believe such a concept.
Alk’ost Saviour System

”I used to be beautiful, but my heart fell. I remain for all of yours.”

In truth, few are settlements that fail under assault.
Despite the turn abuse of Alk’ost machines, they are oftentimes frail, and their cores cannot recover from death.
This is preventable, however, as this specimen carries with them formidable knowledge as unique as themselves.
Bearing a commonly observed number of twenty limbs, it instead only needs two to carry itself, as it no longer has a full core to attend for.
Its remnants must feed on the spoils of other Alk’ost machines, adequately fixing most, if not all damage inflicted on a core, as long as it may still breathe or flow.
Due to its usefulness, many folks seek to destroy it once more, as one time wasn’t enough.
For this reason, its dexterity and stealth are almost unmatched by its peers, learned from too many close calls. It weighs on the heart.
As the Saviour System is uniquely impaired, it must dedicate a pack of six limbs to an action, instead of the usual four. It stands second in pack requirement compared to other Alk’ost machines, as the one with highest pack requirement needs ten limbs for a single action.
Alk’ost Sacrosanct Mainframe
A mere folks tale of a supposedly perfect Alk’ost machine. Considering their inherent nature, it would be unlikely that their kind be perfected, and yet not replicated. Why manufacture so many different, failure-prone instances when you have an apodictic blueprint?
This myth was birthed from knowledge acquired from dead cores, a select few of them preserved without harm to their internals. Apart from a grossly unrecognizable body, there was found perceptions of other machines already known of, along with an intruder in that information, supposedly possessing hundreds and hundreds of limbs, multiple cores, and something unique to them, as each Alk’ost does.
The village is never specified, and it is said the cores are now gone, if they ever existed at all.

”Either the S.O.S. was far too delayed, or the rampage was lightning fast. Regardless, you see it half-idle, picking out meat and foundation methodically. Its long, thin legs makes it tower far over you, but the core is no taller than you.
You make one sound too many. It freezes for half a second, and each arm coldly drops what it was holding. While turning its core towards your general direction, its four legs embedded into the ground are joined by four more, and the rest of its arms join eachother in small groups, like muscles.
You don’t expect this encounter to have a victor.”

Writings/Writings-Index.title: Writings Index
Writings/Writings-Index.content:
Alk’ost
You Want To Get Out

Writings/You-Want-To-Get-Out.title: You Want To Get Out
Writings/You-Want-To-Get-Out.content:
You are a small mute lady, walking out of an old, abandoned house.
You want to get out, then realize you forgot something on your way out.
You go through the house again, retracing your steps from the events you remember.
You are led to the upper floor, where you recall a key, hidden under the pillow of a single person bed.
It’s rusty, dirty, and worn out.
It leads to the basement, where you’re certain you left your belongings.
When you open it again, the lights are already on.
There is a trash heap in the middle of the room, next to a flipped workbench.
The room is well-equipped, just like you remember. There were many woodworking and metalworking tools on the bench. The washing machines are still here, too.
You want to inspect the trash, but you get stuck on it.
You can’t move there is a locked storage room to your left the handle rattles the door rattles the door shakes the door bangs the door breaks open your sight is assaulted with blurry red blurry brown blurry pink blurry yellow the door closes you fell backwards. into the staircase.
Your head feels warm and sticky.
Running back upstairs, you lock the door and throw the key behind you.
You can hardly see anything but red, your head is pulsating and your lungs are burning and your legs give out when you’re finally past the front door.
You stumble into the grass, feeling ready to fall asleep, but you refuse and stand back up.
You are a small mute lady, walking out of an old, abandoned house.
You want to get out, then realize you forgot something on your way out.


                  
                  Lore 
                  
                
You are a small lady who has died in her family home.
